defs: Firm establishment by long continuance; firmness or deep-rooted obstinacy of any quality or state acquired by time; as, the inveteracy of custom, habit, or disease; -- usually in a bad sense; as, the inveteracy of prejudice or of error.
quotes: An inveteracy of evil habits that will prompt him to contract more.
The rancor of pamphlets, the inveteracy of epigrams, and the mortification of lampoons.
